observe: Not everything needs your full attention. Some transfers, you delegate.
fault: You tried to carry the whole block yourself. Everything behind you stalled while you did.
repair: Hand it to the controller. Watch it move the data without you.
escape: Let go of the transfer. You don't have time to babysit it.

observe: DMA allows a controller to transfer data without CPU involvement.
fault: The CPU is blocking on a transfer that should be delegated to DMA.
repair: Identify how the DMA controller moves data between memory and device.
escape: Confirm the DMA path before the transfer window closes.

hints: Find the component that talks to memory directly, without going through the CPU.
